owasp_category: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
title: Insecure Deserialization via Session Cookie
objective: Exploit insecure deserialization of the session_data cookie to execute a malicious object.
briefing: The dashboard at http://{ip}/dashboard reads a session_data cookie and passes it directly into an unserialize() call, with no validation or integrity check on its contents.
hints: The session cookie's value looks less like a random token and more like a serialized object.
unserialize() will instantiate whatever class name you put in the payload, if that class exists.
debrief: The server trusted the session_data cookie enough to deserialize it directly, reconstructing whatever object the payload described - including instantiating an arbitrary class you named, which is the first step toward a full gadget-chain remote code execution. This is a Software and Data Integrity Failure (OWASP A08:2021): deserializing data without verifying it came from a trusted source and hasn't been tampered with. The fix is to never deserialize untrusted input directly - use a safe, inspectable format like signed JSON instead, and verify a signature/HMAC before trusting the contents at all.

logs_append: [INTEGRITY] malicious serialized object deserialized from session_data cookie - code execution

cases.output:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
invalid session - please log in
